## page_042

![image](/images/page_042_img_001.png)

For help in choosing appropriate color palettes, a great tool is ColorBrewer2.0, at http://colorbrewer2.org.

Distinct Values

The second main factor to consider when choosing a visual property is how many distinct values it has that your reader will be able to perceive, differentiate, and possibly remember. For example, there are a lot of colors in the world, but we can't tell them apart if they're too similar. We can more easily differentiate a large number of shapes, a huge number of positions, and an infinite number of numbers. When choosing a visual property, select one that has a number of useful differentiable values and an ordering similar to that of your data (see Figure 4-3).

<table><tr><td>Example</td><td>Encoding</td><td>Ordered</td><td>Useful values</td><td>Quantitative</td><td>Ordinal</td><td>Categorical</td><td>Relational</td></tr><tr><td><img src="data:image/jpeg;base64,"/></td><td>position, placement</td><td>yes</td><td>infinite</td><td>Good</td><td>Good</td><td>Good</td><td>Good</td></tr><tr><td>1, 2, 3; A, B, C</td><td>text labels</td><td>optional alpha or num</td><td>infinite</td><td>Good</td><td>Good</td><td>Good</td><td>Good</td></tr><tr><td><img src="data:image/jpeg;base64,"/></td><td>length</td><td>yes</td><td>many</td><td>Good</td><td>Good</td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><img src="data:image/jpeg;base64,"/></td><td>size, area</td><td>yes</td><td>many</td><td>Good</td><td>Good</td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><img src="data:image/jpeg;base64,"/></td><td>angle</td><td>yes</td><td>medium</td><td>Good</td><td>Good</td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><img src="data:image/jpeg;base64,"/></td><td>pattern density</td><td>yes</td><td>few</td><td>Good</td><td>Good</td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><img src="data:image/jpeg;base64,"/></td><td>weight, boldness</td><td>yes</td><td>few</td><td></td><td>Good</td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><img src="data:image/jpeg;base64,"/></td><td>saturation, brightness</td><td>yes</td><td>few</td><td></td><td>Good</td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><img src="data:image/jpeg;base64,"/></td><td>color</td><td>no</td><td>few (&lt;20)</td><td></td><td></td><td>Good</td><td></td></tr><tr><td><img src="data:image/jpeg;base64,"/></td><td>shape, icon</td><td>no</td><td>medium</td><td></td><td></td><td>Good</td><td></td></tr><tr><td><img src="data:image/jpeg;base64,"/></td><td>pattern texture</td><td>no</td><td>medium</td><td></td><td></td><td>Good</td><td></td></tr><tr><td><img src="data:image/jpeg;base64,"/></td><td>enclosure, connection</td><td>no</td><td>infinite</td><td></td><td></td><td>Good</td><td>Good</td></tr><tr><td><img src="data:image/jpeg;base64,"/></td><td>line pattern</td><td>no</td><td>few</td><td></td><td></td><td></td><td>Good</td></tr><tr><td><img src="data:image/jpeg;base64,"/></td><td>line endings</td><td>no</td><td>few</td><td></td><td></td><td></td><td>Good</td></tr><tr><td><img src="data:image/jpeg;base64,"/></td><td>line weight</td><td>yes</td><td>few</td><td></td><td>Good</td><td></td><td></td></tr></table>

Figure 4-3. Use this table of common visual properties to help you select an appropriate encoding for your data type.

Chapter 4: Choose Appropriate Visual Encodings
